sectionOrderRules: Hero must signal authority and reserve, not enthusiasm — confident headline, no exclamation marks, restrained imagery.
Expertise areas must be presented as a taxonomy (groups of practice areas), not a flat services list, so the visitor self-qualifies into the right partner.
Credentials must list bar admissions, professional bodies or audit registrations the firm holds, with the issuing authority named.
Contact CTA should read as 'Boka introduktionssamtal' or similar measured language, never 'Skicka iväg!'.

Practice grid must group practice areas under recognisable headings (e.g. Bolagsrätt, Skatt, Tvistelösning) and give each area a one-sentence scope description that a buying-side counsel can quote internally.
Industries served acts as a second axis that lets specialised buyers (banking, life sciences, real estate) recognise relevant precedent.
Story must be specific to the firm — founding year, lead partners, professional positioning — not generic 'we believe in client value' filler.
Partners grid must list at least one named partner with credential and a direct contact line so a serious enquiry can land in the correct inbox.
Direct phone numbers and email addresses must be tel:/mailto: links so a senior buyer can act in two clicks.
Multi-office firms should list each office address as its own readable block so a prospect can choose the relevant location.
